verification_gate: Red tests fail first for: Trigger + idempotency defined; Ordering vs host boot set; No host-service coupling; green only when all pass with a recorded proof record.

gate_result.trigger_evidence: planning-spine-v0/docs/lifeos-reattach.service is a declarative systemd USER unit: WantedBy=default.target (fires at user login/boot session start), Type=oneshot invoking the idempotent reattach command; the engine emits the identical unit deterministically.
gate_result.ordering_evidence: The unit orders only within the user session — no After= on any host service/target.
gate_result.coupling_evidence: No Requires=/BindsTo= on any host system unit (asserted line-by-line): the host boots and updates freely (G1) while the unit re-attaches LifeOS.
gate_result.product_execution_evidence: The unit the engine emits is byte-compared to the published artifact.
gate_result.red_first: gates committed red with the re-attach engine and unit absent (4/4 files failing), green at HEAD (lane 12/12)

proof_summary: ARCHBP-093 is complete: a login/boot-triggered, idempotent systemd USER unit re-materializes the envelope and re-attaches state, ordered only within the user session and coupled to no host system service — the host lifecycle stays free.
